tuv.seg: Nkandziyiso wa 2019 wa Makumu ya Lembe

Abbreviations and Acronyms. Abbreviations and acronyms like “USA” or “ICE” should be avoided in the source text because they may be read in a way that does not coincide with their spelling. Additionally, there may be multiple accurate readings for a single abbreviation. For example, the acronym “ICE” could be pronounced “I-C-E” or as a single word.

Swipfuxeto na Swihlamusela-marito. Swipfuxeto na swihlamusela-marito swo fana na “USA” kumbe “ICE” swi fanele ku papalatiwa eka tsalwa ra xihlovo hikuva swi nga hlayiwa hi ndlela leyi nga fambisaniki na mapelo ya swona. Ku engetela kwalaho, ku nga ha va ni ku hlayiwa ko tala loku kongomeke ka xifunengeto xin’we. Hi xikombiso, xihlamusela-marito lexi nge “ICE” xi nga vitaniwa “I-C-E” kumbe tanihi rito rin’we.
<accuracyLink>Vona swo tala eka swipimelo swa ku pakanisa.</accuracyLink>
{ $actionType }<playIcon></playIcon> did they accurately speak the sentence?
{ $actionType }<recordIcon></recordIcon> kutani u hlaya xivulwa hi rito leri tlakukeke
Add a language you want to contribute to
Engetela ririmi leri u lavaka ku hoxa xandla eka rona
Add as much information as you can about the language. This could include:
Engetela vuxokoxoko byo tala hilaha u nga kotaka hakona mayelana ni ririmi. Leswi swi nga katsa:
Ku engetela tindzimi to tirha hi tona
Engetela <wikipediaLink>ndhawu ya mani na mani</wikipediaLink> swivulwa

All sentences you submit must be under <wikipediaLink>Public Domain (CC-0) license</wikipediaLink>. To support the inclusion of work not under public licence, we have created a <cc0WaiverLink>Contributions Agreement template</cc0WaiverLink> for works where the copyright owner would like to contribute the material to Common Voice.

Swivulwa hinkwaswo leswi u swi rhumelaka swi fanele ku va ehansi ka <wikipediaLink>Layisense ya Domain ya Mani na Mani (CC-0)</wikipediaLink>. Ku seketela ku katsa ntirho lowu nga riki ehansi ka layisense ya mfumo, hi endlile <cc0WaiverLink>xifaniso xa Ntwanano wa Minyikelo</cc0WaiverLink> ya mintirho laha n’wini wa mfanelo ya vutshila a nga tsakelaka ku hoxa xandla eka swilo eka Common Voice.

All voice clips in the dataset are scrubbed of personally identifying information. When a contributor provides demographic data via their profile, that information is de-identified from their voice clips before being bundled for download in the dataset and is never made public on their profile page.

Swiphemu hinkwaswo swa rito eka dataset swi hlantswiwa hi rungula leri kombisaka munhu. Loko muhoxa xandla a nyika datha ya nhlayo ya vaaki hi ku tirhisa phurofayili ya vona, vuxokoxoko byebyo byi susiwa eka swiphemu swa vona swa rito byi nga si hlanganisiwa ku dawuniloda eka sete ya datha naswona a byi si tshama byi endliwa erivaleni eka tluka ra vona ra phurofayili.

Anonymized user data like age, gender, and accent helps improve the audio data used to train the accuracy of speech recognition engines. Your username and email will never be associated with your submitted data, and you can choose whether to make your username public or anonymous.

Data ya vatirhisi leyi nga fihliwa yo fana na malembe, rimbewu, na mavulavulelo yi pfuneta ku antswisa data ya mpfumawulo leyi tirhisiwaka ku letela ku pakanisa ka tinjhini to tiva mbulavulo. Vito ra wena ra mutirhisi na imeyili a swi nge pfuki swi fambelanisiwile na data ya wena leyi rhumeriweke, naswona u nga hlawula loko u ta endla vito ra wena ra mutirhisi ri va ra mani na mani kumbe ri nga tiviwi.

A quiet background hubbub is OK, but we don’t want additional voices that may cause a machine algorithm to identify words that are not in the written text. If you can hear distinct words apart from those of the text, the clip should be rejected. Typically this happens where the TV has been left on, or where there is a conversation going on nearby.

Mpfumawulo wa le ndzhaku lowu rhuleke wu lulamile, kambe a hi lavi marito lama engetelekeke lama nga endlaka leswaku algorithm ya muchini yi vona marito lama nga riki kona eka tsalwa leri tsariweke. Loko u kota ku twa marito yo hambana handle ka ya tsalwa, xiphemu xi fanele ku ariwa. Hi ntolovelo leswi swi humelela laha TV yi tshikiweke yi pfuriwile, kumbe laha ku nga na mbulavurisano lowu yaka ekusuhi.
A voice clip is marked "valid" when a user gives it a Yes vote.
Xiphemu xa rito xi funghiwa "xa tirha" loko mutirhisi a xi nyika vhoti ya Ina.
Avoid too much background noise - it should be easy to hear you.
Papalata mpfumawulo wo tala wa le ndzhaku - swi fanele swi olova ku ku twa.

Be cautious before rejecting a clip on the ground that the reader has mispronounced a word, has put the stress in the wrong place, or has apparently ignored a question mark. There are a wide variety of pronunciations in use around the world, some of which you may not have heard in your local community. Please provide a margin of appreciation for those who may speak differently from you.

Vana ni vuxiyaxiya u nga si ala xiphemu laha muhlayi nga vula rito hi ndlela leyi nga tolovelekanga, a nga veka ntshikilelo endhawini leyi hoxeke kumbe swi tikomba onge u honise mfungho wa xivutiso. Ku ni mavitanelo yo hambana-hambana lama tirhisiwaka emisaveni hinkwayo, lawa man’wana ya wona ma nga ha vaka u nga ma twanga emugangeni wa ka n’wina. Hi kombela u hlonipha lava vuluvulaka hi ndlela leyi hambaneke na wena.
[‘Beret’ is OK whether with stress on the first syllable (UK) or the second (US)]
[‘Beret’ yi lulamile hambi hi ku tshikileriwa eka xitwari xo sungula (UK) kumbe xa vumbirhi (US)]
<bold>Hi pfuneni</bold> ku kuma marito yo tala
Why an email? We may need to contact you in the future about changes to the dataset, an email provides us a point of contact.
Hikokwalaho ka yini imeyili? Hi nga lava ku tihlanganisa na wena eka nkarhi lowu taka mayelana ku cinca ka datasete, imeyili yi hi nyika ndhawu yo hlangana.
By editing your goal, you may lose your existing progress.
Hi ku hlela pakani ya wena, u nga ha lahlekeriwa hi nhluvuko wa wena lowu nga kona.
By opting in to receive emails you state that you are okay with Mozilla handling this info as explained in Mozilla’s <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>.

Hi ku hlawula ku nghena ku amukela ti-imeyili u vula leswaku u kahle hi Mozilla ku khoma vuxokoxoko lebyi tanihi laha swi hlamuseriweke hakona eka <privacyLink>Pholisi ya Vuhlayiseki ya Mozilla</privacyLink>.
You agree to not attempt to determine the identity of speakers in the Common Voice dataset
Wa pfumela ku nga ringeti ku kumisisa vutivi bya swivulavuri eka rungula ra Common Voice
By providing some information about yourself, the audio data you submit to Common Voice will be more useful to Speech Recognition engines that use this data to improve their accuracy.

Hi ku nyika vuxokoxoko byo karhi mayelana na wena, data ya mpfumawulo leyi u yi rhumelaka eka Common Voice yi ta pfuna swinene eka tinjhini to Lemuka Rito leti tirhisaka datha leyi ku antswisa ku pakanisa ka tona.
By using Common Voice, you agree to our <termsLink>Terms</termsLink> and <privacyLink>Privacy Notice</privacyLink>
Hi ku tirhisa Common Voice, u pfumelelana na hina <termsLink> Minkarhi</termsLink> na <privacyLink>Xitiviso xa Vuhlayiseki</privacyLink>
A wu swi koti ku endla xiboho?
Change your email via Settings under Login Identity
Cinca imeyili ya wena hi ku tirhisa Switirhisiwa ehansi ka Vutivi bya ku Nghena
Check if your language is listed on the Common Voice Languages page. If not, fill out this form and we’ll send you a handy email explaining how to get your language set up.

Languta loko ririmi ra wena ri xaxametiwile eka Pheji ra Tindzimi ta Common Voice. Loko swi nga ri tano, tata fomo leyi hi ta ku rhumela imeyili leyi pfunaka ku hlamusela ndlela yo endla leswaku ririmi ra wena ri vekeriwa.
{ $clipCount } voice clips, total archive size { $size }. Expires { $expires }.
{ $clipCount } swiphemu swa rito, mpimo hinkwawo wa akhavhiyu { $size }. Ku hela nkarhi { $expires }.
Swiphemu Leswi U nga swi Tiyisisa
Collecting sentences from the public domain, or writing new ones for the public domain.
Ku hlengeleta swivulwa eka ndhawu ya mani na mani, kumbe ku tsala swintshwa eka ndhawu ya mani na mani.
Common Voice data plus all other voice datasets above.
Rungula ya Common Voice ku engetela eka tidathaseti tin’wana hinkwato ta rito leti nga laha henhla.

Common Voice is a crowdsourcing platform, and the languages were all added by volunteers. We would love for you to add your language! <languageRequestLink>Ask about adding your language.</languageRequestLink>

Common Voice i pulatifomo leyi kumaka swihlovo swa ntshungu, naswona tindzimi hinkwato ti engeteriwile hi vatirhi vo tirhandzela. Hi nga tsakela leswaku u engetela ririmi ra wena! <languageRequestLink>Vutisa hi ku engetela ririmi ra wena.</languageRequestLink>
Common Voice is Mozilla's initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Common Voice i kungu ra Mozilla ro pfuneta ku dyondzisa michini ndlela leyi vanhu va xiviri va vulavulaka hi yona.
Common Voice is part of Mozilla's initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Common Voice i xiphemu xa kungu ra Mozilla ro pfuneta ku dyondzisa michini ndlela leyi vanhu va xiviri va vulavulaka ha yona.
Common Voice is the world’s largest publicly available, multi-language voice dataset.
Common Voice i dataset ya rito ya tindzimi to tala leyi kumekaka erivaleni emisaveni hinkwayo.

Common Voice recordings are used by academics, small businesses, and voice recognition enthusiasts to help train and grow publicly available resources like voice models.
Can you let us know why you would like your recordings deleted?

Swi rhekhodo swa Common Voice swi tirhisiwa hi swidyondzeki, mabindzu lamatsongo, na lava tsakelaka ku tiva rito ku pfuneta ku letela na ku kurisa switirhisiwa leswi kumekaka erivaleni ku fana na timodeli ta rito.
Xana u nga hi tivisaxivangelo xo lava leswaku swi rhekhodo swa wena swi susiwa?
Ku nghenelela ka vaaki na ku endla swiboho.
Content available under a <licenseLink>Creative Commons license</licenseLink>
Vuxokoxoko byi kumeka ehansi ka <licenseLink>Layisense ya Creative Commons</licenseLink>
Hoxa Xandla Hi Rito Ra Wena
Contributors record voice clips by reading from a bank of donated sentences.
Va hoxa xandla va rhekhoda swiphemu swa rito hi ku hlaya ebangi ya swivulwa leswi nyikeriweke.
<coquiLink>Coqui</coquiLink> is dedicated to open speech technology. Their projects include deep learning based STT and TTS engines.
<coquiLink>Coqui</coquiLink> yi tinyiketerile eka thekinoloji ya mbulavulo lowu pfulekeke. Tiphurojeke ta vona ti katsa tinjhini ta STT na TTS leti simekiweke eka dyondzo yo enta.
A swi koteki ku engetela ririmi

A swi koteki ku susa ririmi: ririmi a ri kumeki
{ $countOfReviewed } sentences are already reviewed. Great job!
{ $countOfReviewed } swivulwa se swi pfuxetiwile. Ntirho lowukulu!
{ $countOfUnreviewed } sentences are unreviewed. If you want, you can also review your sentences now before submitting them.
{ $countOfUnreviewed } swivulwa a swi kamberiwanga. Loko u swi lava, u nga ha tlhela u kambisisa swivulwa swa wena sweswi u nga si swi rhumela.
Creating opportunities for a diversity of people to contribute to Common Voice ensures the dataset serves as many people as possible. We’ve created resources and templates that you can use!

Ku tumbuluxa swivandlanene swa vanhu vo hambana ku hoxa xandla eka Common Voice swi tiyisisa leswaku sete ya datha yi tirhela vanhu vo tala hilaha swi nga kotekaka hakona. Hi endlile switirhisiwa na tithempleyiti leti u nga ti tirhisaka!
Susa swilo leswi ndzi swi rhekhodiweke
Lahla ku rhekhodiwa loku yaka emahlweni
Don't see your language on Common Voice yet?
A wu se vona ririmi ra wena eka Common Voice?
Don’t see your language reflected in the Dataset? To request a language head over to our Languages page.
A wu voni ririmi ra wena ri kombisiwa eka Rungula? Ku kombela ririmi kongoma eka tluka ra hina ra tindzimi.
Download the Single Word Target Segment
Downloda Xiyenge xa ​​Rito rin’we leri kongomisiweke
Do you have ideas on how we can make the Common Voice dataset better? Let us know on Discourse
Xana u na miehleketo ya leswaku hi nga endla njhani leswaku rungula ra Common Voice ri antswa? Hi tivise eka Discourse
Xana u lava ku ya emahlweni?
Do you want to Speak, Listen or both?
Xana u lava ku Vulavula, Ku Yingisela kumbe hi vumbirhi bya swona?
Koka u tlhela u hoxa kumbe <browseWrap>Pfula-pfula</browseWrap>
During contribution submission feedback will be skipped after clicking 'Submit'. Contribution will continue directly with the next set of 5 recordings or validations.

Hi nkarhi wa ku rhumela ku hoxa xandla mbuyelo wu ta tluriwa endzhaku ko ku tsopeta 'Rhumela'. Ku hoxa xandla ku ta ya emahlweni hi ku kongoma hi sete leyi landzelaka ya 5 wa tirhekhodo kumbe ku tiyisisiwa.

Each entry in the dataset consists of a unique MP3 and corresponding text file. Many of the { $total } recorded hours in the dataset also include demographic metadata like age, sex, and accent that can help train the accuracy of speech recognition engines. The dataset currently consists of { $valid } validated hours in { $languages } languages, but we’re always adding more voices and languages. Take a look at our Languages page to request a language or start contributing.

Ngheniso wun’wana na wun’wana eka datasete wu na MP3 yo hlawuleka na fayili ya tsalwa leyi fambelanaka na yona. Tiawara to tala leti rhekhodiweke ta { $total } eka datasete ti tlhela ti katsa vuxokoxoko byo karhi ya nhlayo ya vaaki ku fana na malembe, rimbewu, na mavalavulelo leswi nga pfunaka ku letela mpakaniso wa tinjhini ta lemukiso wa mbulavulo. Sweswi datasete yi na { $valid } wa tiawara leti tiyisisiweke hi tindzimi ta { $languages }, kambe hi tshamela ro engetela marito yo tala na tindzimi. Languta eka pheji ra hina ra Tindzimi ku kombela ririmi kumbe ku sungula ku hoxa xandla.
<emailFragment>Sign up</emailFragment> to our mailing list to learn how you can take part in campaigns, events and co-design features on Common Voice.
<emailFragment>Tsarisa</emailFragment> enxaxamelweni wa hina wo posa ku dyondza ndlela leyi u nga hlanganyela eka matsima, swiendlakalo ni swivumbeko swa maakelo-kulorhi eka Common Voice.
Email is already used for a different account
Imeyili se yi tirhisiwa eka akhawunti leyi hambaneke
Enter email to send a sign up link
Nghenisa Imeyili ku rhumela link yo tsarisa
Error while fetching rejected sentences. Please try again.
Xihoxo loko u ri karhi u teka swivulwa leswi ariweke. Hi kombela u ringeta nakambe.
Error while fetching your sentences. Please try again.
Xihoxo loko u ri karhi u teka swivulwa swa wena. Hi kombela u ringeta nakambe.

Expert linguists can also help, try to reach out to local linguistic universities (both teachers and students) and see if they can help to gather a diverse set of sound-diverse sentences in your language.

Vativi va tindzimi va nga tlhela va pfuna, va ringeta ku fikelela tiyunivhesiti ta tindzimi ta laha kaya (vadyondzisi na swichudeni hi vumbiri ka vona) na ku vona loko va nga pfuneta ku hlengeleta sete yo hambana ya swivulwa swo hambana swa mpfumawulo eka ririmi ra wena.
Failed to delete selected sentences… Please try again!
Ku tsandzekile ku susa swivulwa leswi hlawuriweke... Hi kombela u ringeta nakambe!
Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required.
Swivandla leswi funghiweke hi xinyeletana (*) swa laveka.
Filtered sentences due to requirements failing (please submit fixed versions as new sentences):
Swivulwa leswi sefiweke hikwalaho ka swilaveko leswi tsandzekaka (hi kombela u rhumela tivhidiyo leti nga cinciki tanihi swivulwa leswintshwa):
Finding existing sentences in the Public Domain
Ku kuma swivulwa leswi nga kona eka Xiyenge xa ​​Mani na Mani

Find others who care. That could be universities, language schools, advocacy groups or data science communities. Reach out and explain clearly how they can help and why. <outreachTemplates>See our template outreach emails</outreachTemplates>.

Kuma van’wana lava nga na maka. Sweswo swi nga va tiyunivhesiti, swikolo swa tindzimi, mintlawa ya vuyimeri kumbe miganga ya sayense ya data. Fikelela mavoko u hlamusela kahle laha va nga pfunaka kona ni leswaku hi yini. <outreachTemplates>Vona ti-imeyili ta hina ta ku fikelela ta xifaniso</outreachTemplates>.

First, check if your language <languageLink>already exists</languageLink>. If it isn’t, you can <languageRequestLink>ask about adding your language</languageRequestLink>. There are two stages. Translating the site, and collecting sentences

Xo sungula, kambela loko ririmi ra wena <languageLink>se ri ri kona</languageLink>. Loko ri nga ri kona, u nga <languageRequestLink>vutisa hi ku engetela hi ririmi ra wena</languageRequestLink>. Ku ni switeji swimbirhi. Ku hundzuluxela sayiti, na ku hlengeleta swivulwa.

Foreign letters. Letters must be valid in the language being spoken. For example, “ж” is a letter in the Russian alphabet but is never used in English and so should never appear in any English source text.

Maletere ya matiko mambe. Maletere ma fanele ma tirha hi ririmi leri vulavuriwaka. Hi xikombiso, “ж” i letere leri nga kona eka maletere ya Xirhaxiya kambe a ri si tshama ri tirhisiwa eka Xinghezi naswona hikwalaho a ri fanelanga ri humelela eka tsalwa rihi na rihi ra xihlovo ra Xinghezi.

For every voice clip donated, and every audio clip validated, your account dashboards are updated to reflect your latest progress in each language you contribute to. Yes, you can contribute to more than one!

Use dashboards to track your stats, see how you're doing alongside others in the community, and set daily or weekly contribution goals.

Eka xiphemu xin’wana na xin’wana xa rito lexi nyikeriweke, na xiphemu xin’wana na xin’wana xa mpfumawulo lexi tiyisisiweke, tideshibhodi ta akhawunti ya wena ti pfuxetiwa ku kombisa nhluvuko wa wena wa sweswinyana eka ririmi rin’wana na rin’wana leri u hoxaka xandla eka rona. Ina, u nga hoxa xandla eka ku tlula yin’we!

Tirhisa tideshibhodi ku landzelela switatimende swa wena, vona ndlela leyi u endlaka hi yona etlhelo ka van’wana emugangeni, na ku veka tipakani ta ku hoxa xandla ka siku na siku kumbe vhiki na vhiki.
For public domain text not available online, you could use academic referencing style e.g Harvard style "Jess (2021) My Public licence poems"
Loko ndzimana yo voniwa hi mani na mani yi nga ri kona eka Internet, u nga tirhisa endlelo ra dyondzo ro kombetela, hi xikombiso, endlelo ra Harvard "Jess (2021) My Public licence poems"

For these launched languages the website has been successfully <localizationGlossaryLink>localized</localizationGlossaryLink>, and has enough <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>sentences collected</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink> to allow for ongoing <speakLink>Speak</speakLink> and <listenLink>Listen</listenLink> contributions.

Eka tindzimi leti ti simekiweke, webusayiti yi <localizationGlossaryLink>humelerile xikaya</localizationGlossaryLink>, naswona yi na <sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink>swivulwa leswi eneleke swi hlengeletiwile</sentenceCollectionGlossaryLink> ku pfumelela minyikelo yo yisa yo <speakLink>Vulavula</speakLink> na ku <listenLink>Yingisela</listenLink>.
Swivutiso Leswi Vutisiwaka Nkarhi Na Nkarhi
<githubLink>NVIDIA NeMo</githubLink>™ is an <docsLink>open-source toolkit</docsLink> for researchers developing state-of-the-art conversational AI models.
<githubLink>NVIDIA NeMo</githubLink>™ i <docsLink>xitirhisiwa xa xihlovo lexi pfulekeke</docsLink> eka valavisisi lava endlaka timodeli ta xiyimo xa le henhla ta AI ya mbulavurisano.
<governanceLink>Hlaya swo tala hi ndlela leyi hi lawuriwaka ha yona</governanceLink>
Government proceedings, books and articles are also great however since the text tends to be a little more formal they are less of a priority.
Maendlelo ya mfumo, tibuku na swihloko na swona i swikulu hambiswiritano tanihileswi tsalwa ri talaka ku va ra xivumbeko nyana a ri ri ra nkoka ngopfu.
Xihoxo xa ririmi kumbe xa mapeletelo.

Ku va ni akhawunti a swi bohi leswaku u hoxa xandla, hambileswi swi pfunaka.

Most of the data used by large companies isn’t available to the majority of people. We think that stifles innovation. So we’ve launched Common Voice, a project to help make voice recognition open and accessible to everyone.

Vunyingi bya data leyi tirhisiwaka hi tikhamphani letikulu a yi kumeki eka vunyingi bya vanhu. Hi ehleketa kuri leswi swi sivela vutumbuluxi. Kutani hi simeke Common Voice, phurojeke yo pfuneta ku endla lemukiso wa rito lowu pfulekeke na ku fikeleriwa hi un’wana ni un’wana.

Most of the data used by large companies isn’t available to the majority of people. We think that stifles innovation. So we’ve launched Project Common Voice, a project to help make voice recognition open to everyone.

"Vunyingi bya data leyi tirhisiwaka hi tikhamphani letikulu a yi kumeki eka vunyingi bya vanhu. Hi ehleketa leswi swivelaka ku endla swilo leswintshwa. Kutani hi humese Project Common Voice, phurojeke yo pfuneta ku endla rito ku xiximiwa loku nga pfuleka eka un’wana ni un’wana."

Most recordings are of people talking in their natural voice. You can accept the occasional non-standard recording that is shouted, whispered, or obviously delivered in a ‘dramatic’ voice. Please reject sung recordings and those using a computer-synthesized voice.

Swifaniso swo tala leswi rhekhodiweke i swa vanhu lava vulavulaka hi rito ra vona ra ntumbuluko. U nga amukela rhekhodo ya nkarhi na nkarhi leyi nga riki yo toloveleka leyi huwelelaka, yi hleveteriwa, kumbe leyi nga erivaleni leswaku yi nyikeriwa hi rito ro ‘hlamarisa’. Hi kombela u ala swilo leswi rhekhodiweke ni leswi tirhisaka rito leri endliweke hi khompyuta.

Most speech databases are trained with an overrepresentation of certain demographics which results in a bias towards <articleLink>male and middle class</articleLink>. Accents and dialects that tend to be under-represented in training data sets are typically associated with groups of people who are already marginalised. Many machines also struggle to understand female voices. This is why in our voice database we want variety!

Tidathabeyisi to tala ta mbulavulo ti leteriwile hi ku yimela ku tlula mpimo ka nhlayo yo karhi ya vaaki leswi endlaka leswaku ku va na ku va na xihlawuhlawu eka <articleLink>xiyimo xa xinuna na xa le xikarhi</articleLink>. Ti-accent na tindzimi leti talaka ku va ti nga yimeriwi ngopfu eka tisethi ta datha ya ndzetelo hi ntolovelo ti fambelanisiwa na mintlawa ya vanhu lava se va nga le tlhelo. Michini yo tala na yona yi lwela ku twisisa marito ya vavasati. Leswi hi swona swi endlaka leswaku eka database ya hina ya rito hi lava ku hambana-hambana!
Mozilla Common Voice is an initiative to help teach machines how real people speak.
Mozilla Common Voice i gingiriko ro pfuneta ku dyondzisa michini ndlela leyi vanhu va xiviri va vulavulaka hi yona.

Mozilla Common Voice is made possible by a diverse community of activists, linguists, data scientists, academics and software engineers from all over the world. The project is stewarded by the Mozilla Foundation.

Mozilla Common Voice yi endliwe ku ri yi koteka hi vaaki vo hambana va valweri, vativi va tindzimi, vativi va sayense ya data, swidyondzeki na vanjhiniyara va software ku suka emisaveni hinkwayo. Phurojeke leyi yi fambisiwa hi Mozilla Foundation.

Mozilla is dedicated to keeping the web open and accessible for everyone. To do that we need to empower web creators through projects like Common Voice. As voice technologies proliferate beyond niche applications, we believe they must serve all users equally. That means investing in more languages and accommodating diverse accents and demographics when building and testing voice technologies. Common Voice is a public resource available to everyone and Mozilla teams and developers around the world are already using it on our own projects as well.

Mozilla yi tinyiketerile ku endla leswaku webu yi tshama yi pfulekile na ku ri yi fikelekeka ka vanhu hinkwavo hi ku olova. Ku endla ku ri leswi swi koteka hi fanele ku nyika vatumbuluxi va webu matimba hi ku tirhisa tiphurojeke to fana na Common Voice. Loko titheknoloji ta rito ti karhi ti andza ku tlula switirhisiwa leswi hlawulekeke, hi tshemba leswaku ti fanele ku tirhela vatirhisi hinkwavo hi ku ringana. Sweswo swi vula ku vekisa eka tindzimi to tala na ku amukela mavulavulelo o hambana na nhlayo ya vaaki loko ku akiwa no kambela thekinoloji ya rito. Common Voice i xitirhisiwa xa mani na mani lexi kumekaka eka un’wana na un’wana naswona mintlawa ya Mozilla na vatumbuluxi se va xi tirhisa eka tiphurojeke ta hina na tona emisaveni hinkwayo.

Mozilla’s open source voice recognition engine Deep Speech can be used to build speech recognition applications. Read our <githubLink>Github overview</githubLink> or join the <discourseLink>DeepSpeech Discourse</discourseLink> to learn how to get started.

Injhini yo tiva rito ya xihlovo lexi pfulekeke ya Mozilla leyi nge Deep Speech yi nga tirhisiwa ku aka switirhisiwa swo tiva mbulavulo. Hlaya <githubLink>nkatsakanyo wa hina wa Github</githubLink> kutani u joyina <discourseLink>DeepSpeech Discourse</discourseLink> ku dyondza ndlela yo sungula.
Xana u lava mpfuno wo karhi hi mavulavulelo?
U lava mpfuno wo karhi hi ku hambana-hambana?
Xana u lava ku downloada rungula ra wena?

Now that you know a little bit more about Common Voice, why not try it out? Click on the microphone icon to start reading sentences aloud.

If you prefer to review other people's voice contributions, click on the play icon. You’ll help confirm that recordings match the sentences written on screen.

Sweswi u tivaka swo tala nyana hi Common Voice, ku sele yini ku ri u yi ringeti? Tsopeta eka xifaniso xa xikurisa-marito leswaku u sungula ku hlaya swivulwa hi rito leri tlakukeke.

Loko u tsakela ku kambisisa minyikelo ya rito ya vanhu van’wana, tsopeta eka xifaniso xa ku tlanga. U ta pfuneta ku amukela swi fambelana na swivulwa leswi nga tsariwa eka xikirini.

Now you can donate your voice to help us build an open-source voice database that anyone can use to make innovative apps for devices and the web. Read a sentence to help machines learn how real people speak. Check the work of other contributors to improve the quality. It’s that simple!

Sweswi u nga nyikela hi rito ra wena ku hi pfuna ku aka dathabeyisi ya rito leyi pfulekeke leyi un’wana na un’wana a nga yi tirhisaka ku endla ti-app ta vutshila ta switirhisiwa na webu. Hlaya xivulwa leswaku u pfuna michini ku dyondza ndlela leyi vanhu va xiviri va vulavulaka ha yona. Languta ntirho wa van’wana va hoxa xandla ku antswisa khwalithi. Swi va swi olovile hi ndlela yoleyo!

Numbers. There should be no digits in the source text because they can cause problems when read aloud. The way a number is read depends on context and might introduce confusion in the dataset. For example, the number “2409” could be accurately read as both “twenty-four zero nine” and “two thousand four hundred nine”.

Tinhlayo. A ku fanelanga ku va ni tinhlayo eka tsalwa ra xihlovo hikuva ti nga vanga swiphiqo loko ti hlayiwa ehenhla. Ndlela leyi nomboro yi hlayiwaka hi yona yi famba hi mongo naswona yi nga ha nghenisa ku pfilunganyeka eka dataset. Xikombiso, nomboro ya “2409” yi nga hlayiwa kahle tanihi havumbirhi bya “makhume-mbirhi-mune ziro nhungu” na “magidi mambirhi na madzana mune nhungu”.
Once logged in you can select your languages from the profile section.
Loko se u nghena u nga hlawula tindzimi ta wena eka xiyenge xa ​phurofayili.

On the other hand, if you think that the reader has probably never come across the word before, and is simply making an incorrect guess at the pronunciation, please reject. If you are unsure, use the skip button.

Hi hala tlhelo, loko u ehleketa leswaku kumbexana muhlayi a nga si tshama a hlangana na rito leri, naswona u endla ntsena ku ringanyeta loku hoxeke eka mavitanelo ya rona, hi kombela u ala. Loko u nga tiyiseki, tsopeta tlula.

Optionally submitted demographic data (e.g. age, gender, language, and accent) will never be made public on your profile, and will not be linked to your account in the dataset. Individual audio clips will be associated with demographic data for the purpose of more accurate analysis - for example, a researcher might want to target a training model to a specific demographic segment.

Data ya nhlayo ya vaaki leyi rhumeriweke hi ku hlawula (xik. malembe, rimbewu, ririmi, na mavalavulelo) a yi nge pfuki yi endliwe erivaleni eka phurofayili ya wena, naswona a yi nge hlanganisiwi na akhawunti ya wena eka dataset. Swiphemu swa mpfumawulo swa munhu hi xiyexe swi ta fambelanisiwa na datha ya nhlayo ya vaaki hi xikongomelo xa nxopaxopo lowu kongomeke swinene - xikombiso, mulavisisi a nga ha lava ku kongomisa modele wa ndzetelo eka xiyenge xo karhi xa nhlayo ya vaaki.
Other people validate those voice clips.
\Vanhu van’wana va tiyisekisa swiphemu sweswo swa rito.
Our community manager will reach out to you to offer extra support, and to let you know if there is already a community working on your language
Mufambisi wa hina wa vaaki u ta fikelela eka wena ku ku nyika nseketelo lowu engetelekeke, ni ku ku tivisa ku ri loko se ku ri kona vaaki lava tirhaka eka ririmi ra wena.

You can view our source sentences in this <githubLink>GitHub folder</githubLink>.

Tsalwa ra hina ra xihlovo ri vumbiwile hi minyikelo ya vahoxi va xandla vo sungula xikan’we na mbulavurisano ku suka eka switshuriwa swa tifilimi swa mani na mani swo fana na <italic>It's a Wonderful Life</italic>.
U nga languta swivulwa swa hina swa xihlovo eka <githubLink>folda leyi ya GitHub</githubLink>.

Swikombelo swo dawuniloda leswi rhekhodiweke leswi hundzeke

Common Voice is a publicly available voice dataset, powered by the voices of volunteer contributors around the world. People who want to build voice applications can use the dataset to train machine learning models.

At present, most voice datasets are owned by companies, which stifles innovation. Voice datasets also underrepresent: non-English speakers, people of colour, disabled people, women and LGBTQIA+ people. This means that voice-enabled technology doesn’t work at all for many languages, and where it does work, it may not perform equally well for everyone. We want to change that by mobilising people everywhere to share their voice.

Common Voice i datasete ya marito leyi kumekaka eka un’wana na un’wana, leyi nyikiweke matimba hi marito vanyikeri vo tinyiketela emisaveni hinkwayo. Vanhu lava lavaka ku aka maendlelo ya marito va nga tirhisa datasete ku letela timodlele to dyondza hi michini.

.

Sweswi, tidathaseti to tala ta rito i ta tikhamphani, leswi sivelaka vutumbuluxi. Tidathaseti ta rito na tona a ti yimeli ngopfu: lava nga vulavuriki Xinghezi, vanhu vantima, lava lamaleke, vavasati na vanhu va LGBTQIA+. Leswi swi vula leswaku thekinoloji leyi tirhisaka rito a yi tirhi nikatsongo eka tindzimi to tala, naswona laha yi tirhaka kona, yi nga ha va yi nga tirhi kahle hi ku ringana eka un’wana ni un’wana. Hi lava ku cinca sweswo hi ku hlengeleta vanhu hinkwako ku avelana marito a vona.
People come and contribute their voices.
Vanhu va ta va ta hoxa xandla hi marito ya vona.
Personal dashboards keep you up-to-date with individual and community progress.
Tidashboard ta munhu hi xiyexe ti ku hlayisa u ri karhi u tiva nhluvuko wa munhu hi xiyexe na wa vaaki.

<playbookLink>Kuma xiletelo lexi pfunaka</playbookLink> eka rendzo hinkwaro ra Common voice, ku suka eka ku endla nchumu wuva wa kwala ekaya ku ya eka matirhiselo ya datasete, xikan’we ni ndlela yo ku ti hlanganisa ni muganga wa hina.

Punctuation. Special symbols and punctuation should only be included when absolutely necessary. For example, an apostrophe is included in English words like “don’t” and “we’re” and should be included in the source text, but it’s unlikely you’ll ever need a special symbol like “@” or “#.”

Swihlawulekisi swa mapeletelo. Swikombiso swo hlawuleka ni swikombiso swa mapeletelo swi fanele swi katsiwa ntsena loko swi laveka hi ku helela. Xikombiso, apostrofi yi katsiwile eka marito ya Xinghezi yo fana na “don’t” na “we’re” naswona yi fanele ku katsiwa eka tsalwa ra xihlovo, kambe a swi tikombi leswaku u ta lava ku tirhisa xikombiso xo hlawuleka xo fana na “@” kumbe “#. ” .

Remember that we need permission to publish those sentences, so always ensure that the text belongs to the <wikipediaLink>public domain</wikipediaLink>. If there is no explicit indication, reach out to the person that the text belongs to and ask if they want to contribute their text to the Public Domain. If they want to, help them go through the <cc0WaiverLink>Contributions Agreement</cc0WaiverLink> process. If they do not want to contribute the text, then you are not allowed to use that text.

Tsundzuka leswaku hi fanele ku kuma mpfumelelo wo kandziyisa swivulwa sweswo, kutani minkarhi hinkwayo tiyisisa leswaku tsalwa i ra <wikipediaLink>ndhawu ya mani na mani</wikipediaLink>. Loko ku nga ri na xikombiso lexi nga erivaleni, fikelela munhu loyi tsalwa ri nga ra yena ku vutisa ku ri a nga lava ku hoxa xandla eka Ndawu ya Manhi na Manhi hi tsalwa ra yena. Loko a swi lava, mu pfuni ku hundza eka endlelo ra <cc0WaiverLink>Ntwanano wa Minyikelo</cc0WaiverLink>. Loko va nga lavi ku hoxa xandla hi tsalwa, kutani a wu pfumeleriwi ku tirhisa tsalwa rero.

Sentences must be reviewed and accepted by two people to be included in Common Voice. You create guidelines for your language <languageGuidelinesLink>here</languageGuidelinesLink>. Sentences must be in the <licenseLink>public domain</licenseLink> and shorter than 15 words. You can ask the owner of a text to make it <waiverLink>CC0 using our waiver process</waiverLink>, and send to us on commonvoice@mozilla.com

Swivulwa swi fanele ku kamberiwa no amukeriwa hi vanhu vambirhi leswaku swi ta katsiwa eka Common Voice. U tumbuluxa swiletelo swa ririmi ra wena <languageGuidelinesLink>laha</languageGuidelinesLink>. Swivulwa swi fanele ku va eka <licenseLink>ndawu ya manhi na manhi</licenseLink> naswona swi koma ku tlula 15 wa marito. U nga kombela n’wini wa tsalwa ku ri endla <waiverLink>CC0 hi ku tirhisa endlelo ra hina ro tshika</waiverLink>, naswona u hi rhumela eka commonvoice@mozilla.com

Some languages have enormous variation in grammar, vocabulary and pronunciation. For this reason, we are <ctaLink>introducing ‘Variants’</ctaLink> in 2022. This gives communities a way to distinguish their languages within the larger dataset.

Tindzimi tin’wana ti na ku hambana lokukulu eka ririmi, ntivomarito na mavitanelo ya marito. Hi xivangelo lexi, hi le ku <ctaLink>ku ngheniseni ‘Variants’</ctaLink> hi 2022. Leswi swi nyika miganga ndlela yo hambanyisa tindzimi ta yona endzeni ka dataset leyikulu.
Someone asks for a language to be added.
Munhu u kombela leswaku ku engeteriwa ririmi.
Something went wrong with reCAPTCHA. Please try again.
Ku humelele swin’wana leswi nga famba kahle hi reCAPTCHA. Hi kombela u ringeta nakambe.
Sorry, Common Voice is running slowly. Thanks for your interest.
Kombela ndzivalelo, Common Voice yi famba hi ku nonoka. Ndza khensa ku tsakela ka n'wina.
Hi rivaleli, ku ni nchumu lowu nga famba kahle
Speak in your normal voice! The way you speak is welcome here - we want your accent as it is, and we want your usual volume, style and intonation.

Vulavula hi rito ra wena leri nga toloveka! Ndlela leyi u vulavulaka hi yona ya amukeleka laha - hi lava mavulavulelo ya wena tani hi leswi yi nga xiswona, naswona hi lava mpfumawulo wa wena wo toloveleka, na xitayela hi ku helela.

Speech is often the most natural way we communicate with each other and voice technologies are bringing that convenience to our computers and mobile devices. We want to empower developers to build amazing voice recognition applications like real-time translators and voice-enabled digital assistants. But right now most of the voice data required to build these kinds of apps is expensive and proprietary. We hope the Common Voice dataset will give developers what they need to innovate and make speech technology available in their own language.
To make voice recognition even more universal, we're collecting voice samples in widely spoken languages as well as those with a smaller population of speakers often underserved by commercial speech recognition services. Publishing a diverse dataset of voices will empower developers, entrepreneurs, and entire speech communities to address this gap themselves.

Ku vulavula hi nomo i ndlela leyi nga toloveleka ya ntumbuluku kutani theknoloji ya rito yi tisa ku olova eka tikhompyuta ni tiselfoni ta hina. Hi lava ku nyika matimba eka vaeindli va switirhisiwa swa tiselfoni ku aka switirhiswa swohlamarisa leswi kotaka ku lemuka rito hi nkarhi wa xiviri na vapfuni va xidijitali lava tirhisaka rito. Kambe sweswi datha yo tala leyi lavekaka ku aka switirhiswa leswi ya durha naswona i ya vulawuri. Hi tshemba leswaku dataset ya Common Voice yi ta nyika vaendli leswi swi lavekaka ku tumbuluxa swilo leswintshwa na ku endla leswaku theknoloji ya mbulavulo yi kumeka hi ririmi ra vona.
Ku endla leswaku ku lemuka ka rito ku va swa misavha hinkwayo, hi hlengeleta swikombiso swa rito hi tindzimi leti vulavuriwaka ngopfu xikan’we na leti nga vulavuriwi ngopfu hikanyingi hi vukorhokeri byo lemuka mbulavulo bya mabindzu. Ku kandziyisa dataset leyi nga na marito yo hambana swi ta nyika matimba eka vatumbuluxi, van’wamabindzu, na miganga hinkwayo ya mbulavulo ku lulamisa xivandla lexi nga kona.
Speech-to-text (STT) technologies convert voice data into text.
Tithekinoloji ta mbulavulo ku ya eka tsalwa (STT) ti hundzula datha ya rito yi va tsalwa.

Do not reject clips where the speaker ‘has an accent’ that is different to your own - this is important for voice recognition to work better for everyone.
U nga ali swiphemu laha muvalavuli a vulavulaka hi 'mpfumawulo wa yena’ lowu hambaneke na wa wena - leswi i swa nkoka leswaku ku lemuka rito ku tirha ku antswa eka un’wana na un’wana.
Get creative! Your language community will be unique, and these are just a few ways to get started.
Kuma vutshila byo tumbuluxa! Ntlawa wa tindzimi wa wena wu ta va wo hlawuleka, naswona leti i tindlela ti nga ri tingani ntsena to sungula.

Sentence Collector is a tool for collecting and validating public domain sentences. You will need to create an account. Then, add your language to your Profile. You can Add sentences or Review previously-added sentences.

Muhlengeleti wa Swivulwa i xitirhisiwa xo hlengeleta na ku tiyisisa swivulwa swa ndhawu ya mani na mani. U ta lava ku endla akhawunti. Kutani, engetela ririmi ra wena eka Profayili ya wena. U nga Engetela swivulwa kumbe u Pfuxeta swivulwa leswi engeteriweke khale.
[Sneeze] The giant dinosaurs of the [cough] Triassic.

Swipa kuya exineneni ku pfumelela xivulwa. Swipa kuya eximatsini ku ala. Swipa kuya ehentlha ku yi tlula. U nga rivali ku rhumela nkambisiso wa wena!

Ku ringeta ko hlayanyana ku hlaya rito.
Tatoeba is a large database of sentences, translations, and spoken audio for use in language learning. This download contains spoken English recorded by their community.

Tatoeba i databesi leyikulu ya swivulwa, mihundzuluxulo na vukandziyisi bya mbulavulo bya ku tirhisiwa eka dyondzeni ka ririmi. Fayili leyi nga dowulodiwa yi katsa Xinghezi xo vulavuriwa lexi rhekhodiweke hi muganga wa vona.

Thanks to contributions from over 259k people in over 50 languages, this data is being used to train speech-enabled applications to better respond to the human voice.

Hikwalaho ka la va nga hoxa xandla ku suka eka vanhu vo tlula 259k hi tindzimi to tlula 50, datha leyi yi tirhisiwa ku letela switirhisiwa leswi pfunaka ku vulavula ku hlamula ku antswa eka rito ra munhu.

Thank you for your interest in contributing to { $lang }. We work hard to get every language ready for launch and keep the teams updated via email. If you want to contribute, please provide your email below.

Ndza khensa ku tsakela ka n'wina ku hoxa xandla eka { $lang }. Hi tirha hi matimba ku endla leswaku ririmi rin’wana na rin’wana ri lunghekela ku sunguriwa no hlayisiwa swipano swi pfuxetiwile hi ku tirhisa imeyili. Loko u lava ku hoxa xandla, hi kombela u nyika email ya wena laha hansi.
Thank you! You’ve sent a new language enquiry
Inkomu! U rhumele xivutiso xa ririmi lerintshwa.
The best sources you can look for are podcasts, transcripts, movie scripts and anything that potential can contain everyday conversations.
Swihlovo swa nkoka ngopfu u nga swi langutaku i ti podcast, mpfumawulo, maphepha ya tifilimi naswona xin’wana ni xin’wana lexi nga pfunaka ku va emabulwini ya siku na siku.

The Clip Graveyard consists of voice clips that didn't make it into the Common Voice dataset. Just like the dataset, the Clip Graveyard is available for download.
We would like to thank the following people and organizations for their help with the project:

Xiphemu xa Graveyard xi na swiphemu swa rito leswi nga leswi nga nghenanga eka dataset ya Common Voice. Ku fana na dataset, xiphemu xa Graveyard lexi kumekaka ku xi kopa.
Hi tsakela ku khensa vanhu na tinhlengeletano leti landzelaka eka mpfuneto wa vona eka phurojeke leyi:
The clip has disrespectful or offensive language.
Xivulwa lexi xi na ririmi ro pfumala xichavo kumbe leri khunguvanyisaka.

The Common Voice dataset complements Mozilla’s open source voice recognition engine Deep Speech. The first version of Deep Speech was released in November 2017 and has continued to evolve ever since. Together with the Common Voice dataset, we believe this open source voice recognition technology should be available to everybody. It’s our hope these technologies will enable developers to build a wave of innovative products and services.

Dataset ya Common Voice yi tatisela injhini ya ku tiva rito ya xihlovo lexi pfulekeke xa Mozilla Deep Speech.Vuhundzuluxeri byo sungula bya Deep Speech byi humesiwe hi Nyenyankulu 2017 naswona byi ye emahlweni hi ndzundzuluko ku sukela kwalaho. Xikan’we na dataset ya Common Voice, hi tshemba leswaku theknoloji leyi ya ku tiva rito ya xihlovo lexi pfulekeke yi fanele ku kumeka eka un’wana na un’wana. I ntshembo wa hina leswaku titheknoloji leti ti ta endla ku ri vatumbuluxi va kota ku aka swikumiwa na vukorhokeri bya vutumbuluxi hi vuningi.
The Common Voice Dataset contains hundreds of thousands of voice samples that help developers build voice recognition tools.
Rungula ra Common Voice ri na madzana ya magidi ya swikombiso swa rito leswi pfunaka vaendli ku aka switirhisiwa swo tiva rito.

The Common Voice dataset is an open and publicly available resource that can be used to train a wide variety of speech-enabled applications. To protect the security of our contributors, we ask everyone who downloads the Common Voice dataset to respect contributors’ privacy.
All voice clips in the dataset are scrubbed of personally identifying information. When you download the dataset, you agree to not attempt to determine the identity of any contributor. That means you cannot try to link information in the dataset to a contributor’s personal information. You may, however, use the dataset to train speech recognition, speaker recognition, or other applications, by, for instance, linking information in the dataset to other information already in the dataset.

Datasete ya Common Voice i xitirhisiwa lexi nga pfuleka no kumeka erivaleni lexi nga tirhisiwaka ku dyondisa switirhisiwa swo hambana hambana leswi pfunaka ku vulavula. Ku sirhelela vuhlayiseki bya vahoxi va xandla va hina, hi kombela un’wana na un’wana loyi a dawuniloda dataset ya Common Voice ku xixima vuhlayiseki bya vahoxi va xandla. Swiphemu hinkwaswo swa rito eka dataset swi hlantswiwa hi rungula leri kombisaka munhu. Loko u kopa dataset, u pfumela leswaku u nga ringeti ku kumisisa vuxokoxoko bya muhoxaxandla wihi na wihi. Sweswo swi vula leswaku a wu nge ringeti ku hlanganisa rungula leri nga eka dataset ni rungula ra munhu hi xiyexe ra muhoxa xandla. U nga ha, hambiswiritano, tirhisa dataset ku letela ku tiva mbulavulo, ku tiva swivulavuri, kumbe switirhisiwa swin'wana, hi xikombiso, ku hlanganisa mahungu eka dataset na mahungu man'wana lawa se ya nga kona eka dataset.

The Common Voice dataset is available for download under the <licenseLink>CC0</licenseLink> license on <datasetLink>our Datasets page</datasetLink>. You can also download several other publicly available datasets from the same page.

Dataset ya Common Voice yi kumeka ku kopiwa ehansi ka layisense ya <licenseLink>CC0</licenseLink> eka <datasetLink>pheji ya hina ya Datasets</datasetLink>. U nga ha tlhela u kopa tidathaseti tin’wana to hlayanyana leti kumekaka erivaleni eka tluka rin’we.

Muhlengeleti wa Swivulwa swa Rito leri Tolovelekeke u hlengeletile swivulwa swa { $sentenceCount } hi tindzimi ta { $languageCount }!
The count of voice recording hours that have been validated by 2 out of 3 users with a vote of “Yes”. These mark progress toward the overall project 10k hours goal.
Nhlayo ya tiawara to rhekhoda rito leti tiyisisiweke hi vatirhisi va 2 eka va 3 hi vhoti ya “Ina”. Leswi swi fungha nhluvuko ku ya eka pakani ya tiawara ta 10k ya phurojeke hinkwayo.
The count of voice recording hours we have collected so far.
Nhlayo ya tiawara to rhekhoda rito hi yi hlengelete ku fikela sweswi.

The goal of the Common Voice dataset is to enable anyone in the world to build speech recognition, speaker recognition, or any other type of application that requires voice data. A voice assistant is just one of many types of applications you could use the dataset to build.

Xikongomelo xa dataset ya Common Voice i ku endla leswaku un’wana na un’wana emisaveni a kota ku aka ku tiva ka mbulavulo, ku tiva swivulavuri, kumbe muxaka wun’wana na wun’wana wa xitirhisiwa lexi lavaka datha ya rito. Mupfuni wa rito i yin’wana ntsena ya tinxaka to tala ta switirhisiwa leswi u nga tirhisaka dataset ku swi aka.

The multi-language version of the Common Voice dataset is currently undergoing community supported bundling and cleaning. If you would like to help us bring Common Voice to new languages, go check out the <sentenceCollectorLink>Sentence Collection Tool</sentenceCollectorLink> for adding new sentences to the dataset, and Mozilla <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> for translating the website itself. New languages are added to Common Voice for voice contribution when 5000 approved sentences have been collected.

Vuhundzuluxi bya tindzimi to tala bya dataset ya Common Voice sweswi byi le ku endliweni ka ku hlanganisiwa na ku basisiwa loku seketeriwaka hi vaaki. Loko u lava ku hi pfuna ku tisa Common Voice eka tindzimi letintshwa, famba u languta <sentenceCollectorLink>Xitirhisiwa xo Hlengeleta Swivulwa</sentenceCollectorLink> ku engetela swivulwa leswintshwa eka dataset, na Mozilla <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> ku hundzuluxela webusayiti hi yoxe. Tindzimi letintshwa ti engeteriwa eka Common Voice ku hoxa xandla hi rito loko ku hlengeletiwile 5000 wa swivulwa leswi pfumeleriweke.
The number of recordings and which languages you contribute to will be public.
Nhlayo ya swilo leswi rhekhodiweke ni leswaku hi tihi tindzimi leti u hoxaka xandla eka tona leti nga ta va erivaleni.
The process by which a contributor’s profile information is obscured from their donated voice clips when packaged for download as a part of the dataset.
Endlelo leri hi rona vuxokoxoko bya phurofayili ya muhoxa xandla byi fihliwaka eka swiphemu swa vona swa rito leswi nyikeriweke loko swi pakiwile leswaku swi ta kopiwa tanihi xiphemu xa dataset.

There are a lot of public organizations that might want to collaborate and already have a lot of texts they can donate as public domain. Reach out to local universities, Governments and open source organizations to talk about the project and ask for their help.

Ku na tinhlengeletano to tala ta mfumo leti nga ha lavaka ku tirhisana naswona se ti na matsalwa yo tala lawa va nga ma nyikelaka tanihi erivaleni. Fikelela tiyunivhesiti ta laha kaya, Mimfumo na minhlangano ya xihlovo lexi pfulekeke ku vulavula hi phurojeke na ku kombela mpfuneto wa vona.

There are lots of ways to think about language. For the purposes of speech recognition models, Common Voice suggests focussing on ‘mutual intelligibility’, or ‘can speakers of this language mostly understand one another if they try to?’

Ku ni tindlela to tala to ehleketa hi ririmi. Hi swikongomelo swa timodlele ta lemukiso wa mbulavulo, Common Voice yi ringanyeta ku kongomisa eka ‘ntwisiso wa ku pfumelelana’, kumbe ‘xana vavulavuri va ririmi leri va nga twisisana kahle loko va ringeta?’.
Ku rhekhodiwa a ku lehe ngopfu.
Ku rhekhodiwa a ku rhulile ngopfu.
Ku rhekhodiwa a ku ri komile swinene.

There will be natural variations in volume between readers. Reject only if the volume is so high that the recording breaks up, or (more commonly) if it is so low that you can’t hear what is being said without reference to the written text.

Ku ta va na ku hambana ka ntumbuluko eka vholumo exikarhi ka vahlayi. Ala ntsena loko vholumo yi ri ehenhla swinene lerova rhekhodo ya vona i tshovekaka, kumbe (hi ntolovelo) loko yi ri ehansi swinene lerova u nga swi koti ku twa leswi vuriwaka handle ko kombetela eka tsalwa leri tsariweke.

The Sentence Collector is part of <commonVoiceLink>Common Voice</commonVoiceLink>. It allows contributors to collect and validate sentences created by the community. You can use this tool also to import and clean-up small-to-medium-sized public domain corpus you have found or collected. All sentences need to be Public Domain. Approved sentences are exported every week to the Common Voice repository and are released on the Common Voice website on every new deployment.

Muhlengeleti wa Swivulwa i xiphemu xa <commonVoiceLink>Common Voice</commonVoiceLink>. Yi pfumelela va hoxa xandla ku hlengeleta no tiyisisa swivulwa leswi tumbuluxiweke hi vaaki. U nga tirhisa xitirhisiwa lexi nakambe ku nghenisa na ku basisa khophasi ya domeyini ya mani na mani leyitsongo ku ya eka ya le xikarhi leyi u yi kumeke kumbe ku yi hlengeleta. Swivulwa hinkwaswo swi lava ku va Xiyenge xa ​​Mani na Mani . Swivulwa leswi pfumeleriweke swi rhumeriwa vhiki rin’wana na rin’wana eka vuhlayiselo bya Common Voice naswona swi humesiwa eka webusayiti ya Common Voice eka ku tirhisiwa kun’wana na kun’wana lokuntshwa.

This page gives you an overview of all your submitted sentences. You may also delete already submitted sentences if needed, by marking the checkbox next to it and clicking on "Delete selected sentences" at the bottom. Please only remove sentences if absolutely necessary, for example if you noticed after the fact that a sentence is copyright protected.

Tluka leri ri ku nyika nkatsakanyo wa swivulwa swa wena hinkwaswo leswi u swi rhumeleke. U nga ha tlhela u susa swivulwa leswi se swi rhumeriweke loko swi laveka, hi ku fungha bokisi ro kambela leri nga ekusuhi na rona ivi u tikhoma eka "Susa swivulwa leswi hlawuriweke" ehansi. Hi kombela u susa swivulwa ntsena loko swi laveka hi ku helela, xikombiso loko u xiye endzhaku ka mhaka ya leswaku xivulwa xi sirhelelekile hi mfanelo ya vutshila.

This setting controls your leaderboard visibility. When hidden, your progress will be private. This means your image, user name and progress will not appear on the leaderboard. Note that leaderboard refresh takes ~{ $minutes }min to populate changes.

Xiyimiso lexi xi lawula ku vonakala ka huvo ya varhangeri ya wena. Loko u tumbetiwe, nhluvuko wa wena wu ta va wa xihundla. Leswi swi vula leswaku xifaniso xa wena, vito ra mutirhisi na nhluvuko a swi nge humeleli eka bodo ya varhangeri. Xiya leswaku ku pfuxetiwa ka huvo ya varhangeri swi teka ~{ $minutes }min ku tata ku cinca.

To make it into the Common Voice dataset, a voice clip must be validated by two separate users.
Ku endla leswaku xi va rungula ra Common Voice, xiphemu xa rito xi fanele ku tiyisisiwa hi vatirhisi vambirhi lava hambaneke.

To make the Common Voice dataset as useful as possible we have decided to only allow source text that is available under a Creative Commons (CC0) license. Using the CC0 standard means its more difficult to find and collect source text, but allows anyone to use the resulting voice data without usage restrictions or authorization from Mozilla. Ultimately, we want to make the multi-language dataset as useful as possible to everyone, including researchers, universities, startups, governments, social purpose organizations, and hobbyists.

Ku endla leswaku dataset ya Common Voice yi pfuna hinkwako ku kotekaka hi teke xiboho xo pfumelela tsalwa ra xihlovo leri kumekaka ehansi ka layisense ya Creative Commons (CC0) ntsena. Ku tirhisa mpimanyeto wa CC0 swi vula leswaku swa tika swinene ku kuma na ku hlengeleta tsalwa ra xihlovo, kambe swi pfumelela mani na mani ku tirhisa datha ya rito leyi humelelaka handle ka swipimelo swa matirhiselo kumbe mpfumelelo ku suka eka Mozilla. Eku heteleleni, hi lava ku endla leswaku dataset ya tindzimi to tala yi pfuna hi nkwako laha swi kotekaka kona eka un’wana na un’wana, ku katsa na valavisisi, tiyunivhesiti, swibinndzwana, mimfumo, tinhlengeletano ta xikongomelo xa ntshamisano, na lava tsakelaka ku titsakisa.
{ $totalHours } hours is achievable in just over { $periodMonths } months if { $people } people record { $clipsPerDay } clips a day.
{ $totalHours } tiawara ti fikeleleka hi ku tlulanyana { $periodMonths } tin’hweti loko { $people } ti rhekhodo ta vanhu { $clipsPerDay } swiphemu hi siku.

Variants are a specific form of a language - for example shared by those living in a geography or commmunity. Sometimes these are called dialects.

Dataset ya Rito, Yi Lunghekele ku Dawuniloda

Voice is natural, voice is human. That’s why we’re excited about creating usable voice technology for our machines. But to create voice systems, developers need an extremely large amount of voice data.

"Rito i ra ntumbuluko, rito i ra munhu. Hi yona mhaka leyi hi tsakisiwaka hi ku tumbuluxa rito leri tirhisiwaka theknoloji ya michini ya hina. Kambe ku tumbuluxa tisisiteme ta rito, nhlayo leyikulu swinene ya rito datha ya laveka."
Voice is natural, voice is human. That’s why we’re fascinated with creating usable voice technology for our machines. But to create voice systems, an extremely large amount of voice data is required.

Rito i ra ntumbuluko, rito i ra munhu. Hi yona mhaka leyi hi nyanyulaka hi ku tumbuluxa rito leri tirhisiwaka thekinoloji ya michini ya hina. Kambe ku tumbuluxa tisisiteme ta rito, nhlayo leyikulu swinene ya rito data ya laveka.

Voice recognition technology is revolutionizing the way we interact with machines, but the currently available systems are expensive and proprietary. Common Voice is part of Mozilla’s initiative to make voice recognition technologies better and more accessible for everyone. Common Voice is a massive global database of donated voices that lets anyone quickly and easily train voice-enabled apps in potentially every language.

Theknoloji yo tiva rito yi hundzula ndlela leyi hi tirhisanaka hi yona ni michini, kambe tisisiteme leti nga kona sweswi ta ku durha naswona ti ni vulawuri. Common Voice i xiphemu xa kungu ra Mozilla ro endla leswaku theknoloji yo tiva rito yi antswa no fikeleleka eka un’wana na un’wana. Common Voice i dathabeyisil eyikulu ya misava hinkwayo ya marito lama nyikeriweke leyi pfumelelaka un’wana na un’wana ku letela ti-app leti tirhisaka rito hi ririmi rin’wana na rin’wana leri nga kotekaka hi xihatla no ku olova.

Voice recognition technology is revolutionizing the way we interact with machines, but the currently available systems are expensive and proprietary. Mozilla Common Voice is an initiative to make voice recognition technologies better and more accessible for everyone. Common Voice is a massive global database of donated voices that lets anyone quickly and easily train voice-enabled apps in potentially every language.
We're not only collecting voice samples in widely spoken languages but also in those with a smaller population of speakers. Publishing a diverse dataset of voices will empower developers, entrepreneurs, and communities to address this gap themselves.

Theknoloji yo tiva rito yi le ku hundzuleni ka ndlela leyi hi tirhisanaka na michini, kambe tisisiteme leti nga kona sweswi ta durha naswona ti na vulawuri. Mozilla Common Voice i kungu ro endla leswaku theknoloji yo tiva rito yi antswa no fikelela vanhu van'wana na van'wana. Common Voice i dathabeyisi leyikulu leyi nga kona emisaveni hinkwayo ya lava nga nyikela hi marito lama pfumelelaka un’wana ni un’wana ku letela ti-app leti tirhisaka rito hi ririmi leri nga ha vaka ri ri kona hi ku hatlisa ni ku olova.
A hi hlengeleti swikombiso swa rito hi tindzimi leti vulavuriwaka ngopfu ntsena kambe na le ka leti nga na nhlayo yitsongo ya vavulavuri. Ku kandziyisa dataset yo hambana ya marito swi ta nyika matimba eka vatumbuluxi, van'wamabindzu, na miganga ku lulamisa xivandla lexi hi voxe.
Marito Ya Le Ka Inthanete Sweswi
VoxForge was set up to collect transcribed speech for use with Free and Open Source Speech Recognition Engines.
VoxForge yi simekiwile ku hlengeleta mbulavulo lowu tsariweke ku tirhisiwa na Tinjhini to lemuka mbulavulo wa xihlovo xa mahala na lexi pfulekeke.

We at Mozilla are building a community around voice technology. We would like to stay in touch with updates, new data sources and to hear more about how you're using this data.

Hina a Mozilla hi le ku akeni ka muganga wa vaaki ku rhendzela thekinoloji ya rito. Hi tsakela ku tshama hi ri eku hlanganiseni na swintshuxo, swihlovo leswintshwa swa datha na ku twa swo tala hi ndlela leyi u tirhisaka datha leyi ha yona.
We at Mozilla are building a community around voice technology. We would like to stay in touch with updates, new data sources and to hear more about how you're using this data.

Hina a Mozilla hi le ku akeni ka muganga wa vaaki ku rhendzela thekinoloji ya rito. Hi tsakela ku tshama hi ri eku hlanganiseni na swintshuxo, swihlovo leswintshwa swa datha na ku twa swo tala hi ndlela leyi u tirhisaka datha leyi ha yona.

We believe that large and publicly available voice datasets foster innovation and healthy commercial competition in machine-learning based speech technology. This is a global effort and we invite everyone to participate. Our aim is to help speech technology be more inclusive, reflecting the diversity of voices from around the world.

Hi tshemba leswaku tidathaseti ta rito letikulu na leti kumekaka erivaleni ti kurisa vutumbuluxi na mphikizano wa mabindzu lowu hanyeke kahle eka thekinoloji ya mbulavulo leyi simekiweke eka dyondzo ya michini. Leri i matshalatshala ya misava hinkwayo naswona hi rhamba un’wana ni un’wana ku hlanganyela. Xikongomelo xa hina i ku pfuna theknoloji ya mbulavulo ku va yi katsa hinkwavo, ku kombisa ku hambana ka marito ku suka emisaveni hinkwayo.

We believe that large, publicly available voice datasets will foster innovation and healthy commercial competition in machine-learning based speech technology.
Common Voice’s multi-language dataset is already the largest publicly available voice dataset of its kind, but it’s not the only one.
Look to this page as a reference hub for other open source voice datasets and, as Common Voice continues to grow, a home for our release updates.

Hi tshemba leswaku tidathaseti ta rito letikulu, leti kumekaka erivaleni ti ta kurisa vutumbuluxi na mphikizano wa mabindzu lowu hanyeke kahle eka thekinoloji ya mbulavulo leyi simekiweke eka dyondzo ya michini.
Dataset ya tindzimi to tala ya Common Voice se i dataset ya rito leyikulu swinene leyi kumekaka erivaleni ya muxaka wa yona, kambe a hi yona ntsena.
Languta eka thekinoloji pheji leri tanihi ndhawu ya swikombeto ya tidasete tin’wana ta rito ra xihlovo lexi pfulekeke naswona, tanihi leswi Common Voice yi yaka emahlweni yi kula, kaya ra swintshuxo swa hina swa nkandziyiso.
We calculate hours by estimating the average length of each recording, and then multiplying that number by the total number of recordings across all languages.

We prefer natural/conversational sentences. While phonetic diversity and different words in sentences is important, we are trying to make recording sentences as much fun as possible. Therefore it would be great if you could try to keep your sentences as natural/engaging as possible.

We use a Mozilla tool called <pontoonLink>Pontoon</pontoonLink> for translations. Pontoon has lots of languages, but if it doesn’t have yours you can <pontoonRequestLink>request for your language to be added</pontoonRequestLink>. Then, to make the language available on the Common Voice project, <githubRepoLink>request the new language on GitHub</githubRepoLink>. <localizationFragment>See more on site translation</localizationFragment>.

Kutani, ku endla leswaku ririmi ri kumeka eka phurojeke ya Common Voice, . <githubRepoLink>kombela ririmi lerintshwa eka GitHub</githubRepoLink>. <localizationFragment>Vona swo tala eka vuhundzuluxi bya sayiti</localizationFragment>.

We want speech models to be better at understanding a diverse range of speakers. For this to happen, a voice dataset must represent lots of different people.
Hi lava leswaku swikombiso swa mbulavulo swi antswa eku twisiseni ka swivulavuri swo hambana-hambana. Leswaku leswi swi humelela, dataset ya rito yi fanele ku yimela vanhu vo tala vo hambana.

We want the Common Voice dataset to reflect the audio quality a speech-to-text engine will hear in the wild, so we’re looking for variety. In addition to a diverse community of speakers, a dataset with varying audio quality will teach the speech-to-text engine to handle various real-world situations, from background talking to car noise. As long as your voice clip is intelligible, it should be good enough for the dataset.

Hi lava leswaku dataset ya Common Voice yi kombisa khwalithi ya mpfumawulo leyi injhini ya mbulavulo ku ya eka tsalwa yi nga ta yi twa ekhwatini, hikwalaho hi lava ku hambana-hambana. Ku engetela eka ntlawa wo hambana-hambana wa swipikara, dataset leyi nga ni khwalithi yo hambana-hambana ya mpfumawulo yi ta dyondzisa injhini ya mbulavulo ku ya eka tsalwa ku khoma swiyimo swo hambana-hambana swa xiviri, ku sukela eka ku vulavula endzhaku ku ya eka mpfumawulo wa movha. Loko ntsena xiphemu xa wena xa rito xi twisiseka, xi fanele xi va lexinene ku ringana eka dataset.

We want the machine learning algorithms to able to handle a variety of background noise, and even relatively loud noises can be accepted provided that they don’t prevent you from hearing the entirety of the text. Quiet background music is OK; music loud enough to prevent you from hearing each and every word is not.

Hi lava leswaku tialgorithm to dyondza hi muchini ti kota ku khoma mpfumawulo wo hambana-hambana wa le ndzhaku, naswona hambi ku ri mimpfumawulo leyi tlakukeke swinene yi nga amukeriwa ntsena loko yi nga ku siveli ku twa tsalwa hinkwaro. Vuyimbeleri bya le ndzhaku lebyi rhuleke byi lulamile; vuyimbeleri lebyi tlakukeke lerova byi ku sivela ku twa rito rin’wana ni rin’wana a hi rona.
We will also let our community manager know that you’re interested, so they can give you any extra support or information you might need.
Hi ta tlhela hi tivisa mufambisi wa muganga wa ka hina leswaku wa swi tsakela, kutani va swi kota ku nyika nseketelo lowu engetelekeke kumbe vuxokoxoko lebyi u nga ha byi lavaka.
We will be in touch with more information about how to add your language to Common Voice very soon.
Hi ta tihlanganisa na vuxokoxoko byo tala mayelana na ndlela yo engetela ririmi ra wena eka Common Voice ku nga ri khale swinene.
We will be in touch with more information as it becomes available.
Hi ta tihlanganisa na vuxokoxoko byo tala loko byi ri karhi byi kumeka.
We will not make your email public.
A hi nge endli imeyili ya wena yi humela erivaleni.

We will review your request to remove your voice recordings from the dataset. If your request is approved, we will contact those who have downloaded the dataset and request they remove your voice recordings as well.

Hi ta kambisisa xikombelo xa wena xo susa tirhekhodo ta wena ta rito eka dataset. Loko xikombelo xa wena xi amukeriwile, hi ta tihlanganisa na lava va nga kopa dataset hi kombela leswaku va susa swi rhekhodo swa wena swa rito na swona.
What does it mean that I can’t “determine the identity” of speakers in the Common Voice dataset?
Xana swi vula yini leswaku a ndzi swi koti ku “kumisisa vutivi” bya swivulavuri eka rungula ra Common Voice?
What is a language on Common Voice?
Xana ririmi eka Common Voice i yini?
What issues are you experiencing with this sentence?
Hi tihi timhaka leti u langutanaka na tona hi xivulwa lexi?
What kind of goal do you want to build?
Xana u lava ku aka pakani ya njhani?
What level of audio quality is required for a voice clip to be used in the dataset?
Xana i xiyimo xihi xa khwalithi ya mpfumawulo lexi lavekaka leswaku xiphemu xa rito xi tirhisiwa eka rungula?
What’s inside the Common Voice dataset?
I yini lexi nga endzeni ka dataset ya Common Voice?
Xana i yini lexi nga erivaleni?
What’s the difference between Common Voice and Deep Speech?
Xana ku hambana kwihi exikarhi ka Common Voice na Deep Speech?
When a user rejects a voice clip it returns to the Queue. If rejected a second time, the voice clip is moved to the Clip Graveyard.
Loko mutirhisi a ala xiphemu xa rito xi tlhelela eka Fole. Loko xi ariwa ra vumbirhi, xiphemu xa rito xi yisiwa eSirheni ra Xiphemu.

When listening, check very carefully that what has been recorded is exactly what has been written; reject if there are even minor errors.
Very common mistakes include:

Loko u yingisela, kambela hi vukheta swinene leswaku leswi swi nga rhekhodiwa hi swona leswi nga tsariwa; ala loko ku ri ni hambi ku ri na swihoxo leswitsongo.
Swihoxo leswi tolovelekeke swinene swi katsa:
When will you release Common Voice data in other languages?
Xana u ta humesa rini rungula ra Common Voice hi tindzimi tin’wana?
When you request your recordings, we compile them into one or multiple ZIP files. Here are your past requests:
Loko u kombela tirhekhodo ta wena, hi ti hlengeleta ti va fayili yin’we kumbe to tala ta ZIP. Hi leswi swikombelo swa wena swa nkarhi lowu hundzeke:
Where are these <wikipediaLink>public domain</wikipediaLink> sentences from?
Xana swivulwa leswi swa <wikipediaLink>ndhawu ya mani na mani</wikipediaLink> swi huma kwihi?
Where does the source text come from?
Xana tsalwa ra xihlovo ri huma kwihi?
Which variant of { $language } do you speak?
Hi yihi muxaka wa { $language } lowu u wu vulavulaka?
Ha yini ku ri ni phurofayili?
Hi yini leswi swi ri na mhaka?
Why don’t you ask people to read from books or Wikipedia articles in different languages?
Ha yini u nga kombeli vanhu ku hlaya eka tibuku kumbe swihloko swa Wikipedia hi tindzimi to hambana-hambana?
Why do you need so many different speakers per language?
Hi yini u lava swivulavuri swo tala swo hambana-hambana hi ririmi?
Why is 10,000 validated hours the per language goal for capturing audio?
Hi yini tiawara ta 10 000 leti tiyisisiweke ti ri pakani ya ririmi rin’wana ni rin’wana ro teka mpfumawulo?
Why is Common Voice part of the Mozilla mission?
Ha yini Common Voice yi ri xiphemu xa vurhumiwa bya Mozilla?
Ha yini swi ri swa nkoka?
Why is my language not included yet?
Ha yini ririmi ra mina ri nga si katsiwa?
Why should I sign up for an account?
Ha yini ndzi fanele ndzi tsarisa eka akhawunti?
Would you like to request your voice recordings be deleted too, or do you prefer to keep them in the Common Voice dataset?
Xana u nga tsakela ku kombela leswaku swi rhekhodo swa wena swa rito na swona swi susiwa, kumbe u tsakela ku swi hlayisa eka dataset ya Common Voice?
Yes, send me emails. I’d like to stay informed about the Common Voice Project.
Ina, ndzi rhumeleni ti-imeyili. Ndzi tsakela ku tshama ndzi ri karhi ndzi tivisiwa hi tsima ra Common Voice.
Yes, send me emails. I'd like to stay informed about the progress of this language on Common Voice.
Ina, ndzi rhumeleni ti-email. Ndzi tsakela ku tshama ndzi ri karhi ndzi tivisiwa hi nhluvuko wa ririmi leri eka Common Voice.

Yes, we especially want your voice! Part of the aim of Common Voice is to gather as many different accents as possible so that voice recognition services work equally well for everyone. This means donations from non-native speakers are particularly important.

Ina, ngopfu-ngopfu hi lava rito ra wena! Xiphemu xa xikongomelo xa Common Voice i ku hlengeleta swichayachayana swo tala swo hambana hilaha swi nga kotekaka hakona leswaku vukorhokeri byo tiva rito byi tirha kahle hi ku ringana eka un’wana na un’wana. Leswi swi vula leswaku minyikelo leyi humaka eka lava nga vulavuriki ririmi ra rikwavo i ya nkoka swinene.
You are about to initiate a download of <size>{ $size }GB</size>, proceed?
U le kusuhi no sungula ku dawuniloda ka <size>{ $size }GB</size>, yana emahlweni?
You are prepared to initiate a download of { $size }
U lulamerile ku sungula ku dawuniloda ka { $size }
You can also copy and paste the direct URLs into your favorite download manager. They will expire in 12 hours, but you can come back to this page to generate new ones any time.

U nga ha tlhela u kopa ni ku namarheta ti-URL leti kongomeke eka mufambisi wa wena wo kopa lowu u wu tsakelaka. Ti ta hela endzhaku ka tiawara ta 12, kambe u nga vuya eka tluka leri ku humesa letintshwa nkarhi wihi na wihi.
You can also use Keyboard Shortcuts: { sc-review-form-button-approve-shortcut } to Approve, { sc-review-form-button-reject-shortcut } to Reject, { sc-review-form-button-skip-shortcut } to Skip

{ sc-review-form-button-skip-shortcut }{ sc-review-form-button-skip-shortcut }U nga ha tlhela u tirhisa Swipfuxeto swa Khibhodi: { sc-review-form-button-approve-shortcut } ku Pfumelela, { sc-review-form-button-reject-shortcut } ku Ala, { sc-review-form-button-reject-shortcut } ku ya eka Tlula{ sc-review-form-button-skip-shortcut }
You can choose to make your username public or anonymous.
U nga hlawula ku endla vito ra wena ra mutirhisi ri va ra mani na mani kumbe ri nga tiviwi.

You can go to the <datasetsPage>datasets page</datasetsPage>, select the version and language(s) you want, and download it! The files have <metadataLink>associated metadata</metadataLink>, such as demographic information and validation data. You’ll need to provide an email address to download the dataset.

U nga ya eka <datasetsPage>pheji ya ti-dataset</datasetsPage>, u hlawula vuhundzuluxeri ni ririmi leri u ri lavaka, ivi u ri kopa! Tifayili ti na <metadataLink>metadata leyi fambelanaka na yona</metadataLink>, ku fana na vuxokoxoko bya nhlayo ya vaaki na datha ya ku tiyisisa. U ta lava ku nyika adirese ya imeyili ku ri u ta kota ku kopa dataset.
You can help build a diverse, open-source dataset by creating a Common Voice profile and contributing your voice.
U nga pfuneta ku aka rungula ro hambana, ra xihlovo lexi pfulekeke hi ku tumbuluxa phurofayili ya Common Voice na ku hoxa xandla hi rito ra wena.
You can meet others in the Mozilla language communities by joining <discourseLink>Discourse</discourseLink> for topical conversations, or <matrixLink>Matrix</matrixLink> for quick advice.

U nga hlangana ni van’wana ematikweni ya ririmi ya Mozilla hi ku tihlanganisa ni<discourseLink>Discourse</discourseLink>eka tinhloko-mhaka to vulavurisana, kumbe <matrixLink>Matrix</matrixLink> ya xitsundzuxo hi xihatla.
You cannot request your recordings while another request is already in progress.
A wu nge kombeli swi rhekhodo swa wena loko xikombelo xin’wana se xi ri karhi xi endliwa.
You can request a new takeout of your recordings every { $days } days.
U nga kombela ku tekiwa lokuntshwa ka tirhekhodo ta wena endzhaku ka masiku man’wana na man’wana ya { $days }.

You can run events to help people contribute. It’s easier than you think. You could do it online with a videoconferencing tool, or in person if it’s safe. <eventTemplate>Check out our templates and resources for running events</eventTemplate>.

U nga fambisa swiendlakalo ku pfuna vanhu ku hoxa xandla. Swi olova ku tlula leswi u swi ehleketaka. U nga swi endla eka inthanete hi xitirhisiwa xa ku endliwa ka tivhidiyo kumbe hi xiviri loko swi hlayisekile. <eventTemplate>Languta tithempleyiti ta hina na switirhisiwa swo fambisa swiendlakalo</eventTemplate>.

You can <sentenceCollectorLinkAdd>add small numbers of sentences</sentenceCollectorLinkAdd>, or you can do <bulkImportDocsLink>bulk imports using Github</bulkImportDocsLink>. Remember that sentences need to be <licenseLink>CC0 (or public domain)</licenseLink>, or you can write your own.

U nga <sentenceCollectorLinkAdd>engetela tinomboro letitsongo ta swivulwa</sentenceCollectorLinkAdd>, . kumbe u nga endla <bulkImportDocsLink>ku nghenisa hi xitalo hi ku tirhisa Github</bulkImportDocsLink>. Tsundzuka leswaku swivulwa swi lava ku va <licenseLink>CC0 (kumbe domeyini ya mani kumbe mani)</licenseLink>, kutani u nga tsala swa wena.
You can use the <scraperLink>Sentence extractor</scraperLink> to leverage short sentences from Wikipedia.
U nga tirhisa leswi <scraperLink>Xihlamusela-marito xa xivulwa</scraperLink> ku tirhisa swivulwa swo koma ku suka eka Wikipedia.
You could include the website, e.g "Common Voice - https://commonvoice.mozilla.org/"
U nga katsa webusayiti, e.g "Common Voice - https://commonvoice.mozilla.org/"
You could use academic referencing style, e.g Harvard style "Mozilla (2021) Common Voice. Available at https://commonvoice.mozilla.org/ (Accessed: 15th September 2021)"
U nga tirhisa endlelo ra dyondzo ro kombetela, hi xikombiso, endlelo ra Harvard "Mozilla (2021) Common Voice. Yi kumeka eka https://commonvoice.mozilla.org/ (Accessed: 15th September 2021)"

You could use social media platforms to get the message out. Share posts that explain why it matters, and get in touch with other people talking about issues like language rights, voice AI, or bias in tech. See more advice on <campaignLink>running a social campaign, including content you can re-use</campaignLink>.

U nga tirhisa tipulatifomo ta ti-website to endla xinghana ku humesa rungula. Avelana ti post leti hlamuselaka leswaku hikokwalaho ka yini swi ri swa nkoka, naswona u tihlanganisa na vanhu van’wana va vulavula hi timhaka to fana na timfanelo ta ririmi, AI ya rito, kumbe xihlawuhlawu eka theknoloji. Vona switsundzuxo swin’wana eka <campaignLink>ku fambisa pfhumba ra swa ntshamisano, ku katsa na swilo leswi u nga swi tirhisaka nakambe</campaignLink>.
You have not added any languages yet.
A wu si engetela tindzimi tihi na tihi.
You have not selected any languages. Please go to your <profileLink>Profile</profileLink> to select languages.
A wu hlawulanga tindzimi tihi na tihi. Ndzi kombela u ya eka <profileLink>Phurofayili</profileLink ya wena ku hlawula tindzimi.
U fanele u pfumelela ku nghena eka microphone.
You previously skipped sentences while reviewing. Resetting skipped sentences will show all skipped sentences again. This is independent of the language.

U tlule swivulwa enkarhini lowu nga hundza loko u karhi u kambisisa. Ku tlhentlhisa swivulwa u nga swi tlula ku ta kombisa swivulwa leswi u nga swi tlula hinkwaso nakambe. Leswi swi na vutilawuri eka ririmi.

Your accent is the way you pronounce words. It can be shaped by where you have lived, which other languages you speak and lots of other factors. You can share any information you feel is relevant here.

Mavulavulelo ya wena i ndlela leyi u vitana marito hi yona. Yi nga vumbiwa hi laha u tshameke kona, hi tindzimi tin’wana leti u ti vulavulaka ni swilo swin’wana swo tala. U nga avelana rungula leyi u vonaka i ri na nkoka laha.
Your anonymous voice recordings will remain in the Common Voice dataset. Once you delete your profile you will no longer be able to submit a request to remove your recordings from the dataset

Swi rhekhodo swa wena swa rito leswi nga tiviwiki swi ta tshama eka dataset ya Common Voice. Loko u susa phurofayili ya wena a wu nge he swi koti ku rhumela xikombelo xo susa tirhekhodo ta wena eka dataset
Your daily goal has been created
Pakani ya wena ya siku na siku yi tumbuluxiwile
Ku dawuniloda ka wena ku sungurile.
You’re contributing to a target segment
U hoxa xandla eka xiyenge lexi kongomisiweke
You’re contributing to our first target segment
U hoxa xandla eka xiyenge xa ​​hina xo sungula lexi kongomisiweke
You're currently set to <bold>NOT</bold> receive emails such as goal reminders, my progress updates and newsletters about Common Voice
Sweswi u vekiwile leswaku<bold>U NGA</bold> amukeli ti imeyili to fana na switsundzuxo swa pakani, na swintshuxo swa nhluvuko na swiphephana swa mahungu mayelana na Common Voice
You're currently set to receive emails such as goal reminders, my progress updates and newsletters about Common Voice
Sweswi u vekiwile ku amukela ti imeyili to fana na switsundzuxo swa pakani, swintshuxo swa nhluvuko ya mina na swiphephana swa mahungu mayelana na Common Voice.
Your files are being assembled. Please check again later.
Tifayili ta wena ti le ku hlengeletiweni. Hi kombela u tlhela u languta endzhaku.
Your username and email will not be associated with the published data.
Vito ra wena ra mutirhisi na imeyili a swi nge fambisani na rungula leri kandziyisiweke.
Your weekly goal has been created
Pakani ya wena ya vhiki na vhiki yi tumbuluxiwile
You should receive an email shortly explaining the steps to get your language added to Common Voice
U fanele ku kuma imeyili leyi hlamuselaka hi ku komisa magoza yo endla leswaku ririmi ra wena ri engeteriwa eka Common Voice.
You've helped Common Voice reach <goalPercentage></goalPercentage> of our daily { $goalValue } recording goal!
U pfune Common Voice ku fikelela <goalPercentage></goalPercentage> pakani ya hina ya siku na siku yo rhekhoda { $goalValue }!
You've helped Common Voice reach <goalPercentage></goalPercentage> of our daily { $goalValue } validation goal!
U pfunile Common Voice ku fikelela <goalPercentage></goalPercentage> ya pakani ya hina ya siku na siku ya { $goalValue } ya ku tiyisisa!
You've successfully signed up for contributing to { $language }. Thank you.
U tsarisile hi ndlela leyi humelelaka ku hoxa xandla eka { $language }. Inkomu.
